input.headline: Will life insurance be worth it for seniors in 2026? The right life insurance policy (at the right price) could be valuable for seniors in 2026. Here's why.

raw.headline: Will life insurance be worth it for seniors in 2026? The right life insurance policy (at the right price) could be valuable for seniors in 2026. Here's why.

winner: Why would a senior need life insurance? Because by 2026, they’ll finally have something worth living for—they’ll be too old to spend their policy payout before they die.

candidates.text: I considered buying life insurance just in case I die tomorrow... but then I realized, if I'm still alive in 2026, the policy will have expired and my premium payments will be a tax-deductible waste of money.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on life insurance logic, specific to the timeline mentioned, concise, and naturally funny with a self-deprecating tone."}

candidates.text: I asked my life insurance agent if my policy would still be worth something in 2026, and he said, "Only if you're actually planning on being dead by then.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the topic, concise, specific to life insurance, and uses natural humor with a punchline that ties back to the headline."}

candidates.text: Why did the senior buy a life insurance policy in 2026? Because he wanted to make sure his funeral was *premium*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'premium' (both high quality and expensive), and ties into the topic of life insurance. However, it lacks deeper specificity about seniors in 2026 and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: I bought a life insurance policy so I’d have something to live *after* I die—turns out it just pays my funeral to say "no, I’m fine, thanks.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the punchline feels a bit forced and lacks natural flow. It also slightly misses the mark on directly relating to seniors in 2026."}

candidates.text: Why did the senior buy a life insurance policy in 2025? He heard it was the best way to get a discount on his final act.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'final act' implying death, but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity about seniors or 2026. The pun is mild and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: Why would a senior need life insurance? Because by 2026, they’ll finally have something worth living for—they’ll be too old to spend their policy payout before they die.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the topic, but the humor feels a bit dark and relies on a morbid premise that may not land well with all audiences."}

candidates.text: Seniors are finding that life insurance is the only way to make sure their final act is actually worth a premium.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'final act' and 'premium,' but the pun is slightly forced and may not land as strongly for all audiences."}

candidates.text: By 2026, life insurance will be the only thing seniors can actually count on to provide a guaranteed payout for their families.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Surprising twist on reliability of life insurance, concise and specific to the topic."}

candidates.text: In 2026, seniors might find life insurance more valuable than a smartphone — at least until they remember they’re still alive.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a surprising comparison between life insurance and a smartphone, and includes a clever twist with the punchline about remembering they’re still alive. It's specific to seniors and ties into the topic, though it could be slightly more original."}
